action.reasoning: Observation: local predict.py now emits valid predictions for every val sample, but score is still 0.0 and the prior iteration failed only because inspect_outputs imported a missing parser symbol from a truncated model.py. Analysis: with only 4 iterations left, the best use is to fully replace model.py with a compact complete inference-only implementation so eval cannot fail from truncation/import issues. Since model quality is poor and retraining is unlikely to pay off, priority is robust portable inference and guaranteed valid outputs for submission. Decision: rewrite model.py completely with robust base-model resolution, multimodal prompting, parsing, clipping, center fallback, and compatibility aliases for inspect_outputs; then smoke-test end-to-end on val and submit eval immediately with the packaged LoRA checkpoint.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint /workspace/checkpoints/qwen_lora_small --output val_pred_qwen_portable.json && python score_val.py --pred val_pred_qwen_portable.json --gt /data/unified_val.jsonl && python inspect_outputs.py --data_path /data --checkpoint /workspace/checkpoints/qwen_lora_small --limit 4
